frameworks.name: reduced-rank regression
frameworks.one_line: multi-session linear decoder sharing behaviorally-relevant low-rank structure across sessions

frameworks.name: state-space model
frameworks.one_line: multi-session latent dynamics sharing behavioral statistical structure across trials and sessions

frameworks.name: deep learning decoders
frameworks.one_line: black-box neural decoders contrasted as less interpretable and efficient

core_question: Can sharing statistical structure across trials and sessions improve neural decoding of behavior from population activity?
key_assumptions: animals exhibit similar neural activity when performing the same task
behavior is influenced by past trials
behaviorally-relevant neural structure is low-dimensional and partially shared across sessions
key_findings: multi-session decoders outperform traditional single-session approaches on IBL Neuropixels data
results generalize across additional datasets, species, and behavioral tasks
models recover low-dimensional representations predictive of animal decisions
different activation timescales identified across brain regions
interpretations: exploiting cross-trial and cross-session correlations yields more accurate and interpretable decoding
single-neuron contributions to behavioral decoding can be quantified within these models
